methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $96M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Arbor Energy is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Brad Hartwig that has raised $96M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a universe of 9 comparable companies—1 public entity and 8 private firms—including same-stage peers Watershed, Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier from the $96M total raised proxy, implying a $192M valuation at the next stage milestone. Peer-median analysis benchmarks against four same-sector, same-stage comparables, yielding a median valuation of $175M. The peer set spans $110M to $1.8B, indicating material dispersion driven by outlier performance (Watershed at $1.8B) offset by more tightly clustered mid-range valuations.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a valuation range of $149M (low) to $221M (high), with a midpoint of $184M. This narrow interquartile result reflects reasonable convergence between stage-based progression and peer multiples. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in peer valuations—particularly Watershed's $1.8B outlier—and the limited sample size of directly comparable Series A companies in the Climate & Energy sector. The median of $184M represents the most defensible central estimate absent material new information on Arbor's unit economics or market traction relative to the peer cohort.
